title: veronica daurica subsp. pyrethrina
intro: Veronica daurica subsp. pyrethrina, also known as Pyrethrum, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae, native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia. Traditionally, the plant's flowers have been used for their medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, headaches, and skin conditions due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Veronica daurica subsp. pyrethrina include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and fight off infections, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
